reviews.review_msg: ENSC 120 is just a very easy course to take with her. overall, she's alright, the communication i got back from her for a few assignments i felt was unfair, but since 120 is very easy, its not that hard to make a comeback.

Came in this class hoping it was good because everyone talks well of Atousa but yeah...this ain't it. Tests are straight forward but she doesn't give you anything to study for the tests, homework takes a while to do it's like 7 questions and sometimes with MATLAB, you also have group MATLAB assignments doable alone. Didn't like this class overall..

I'll give it to you straight: everyone likes her  because of 120 and how easy she was in the course. However, this is a 3rd year level course and her lectures are garbage - you need to rely to other sources like videos and textbooks to get anything in this class and only attend lectures for those quiz codes. Tests are harder, altered assignments.
horrible lectures

Genuinely a really nice lady. But unfortunately, her lectures are not too great. After the midterm, the material really accelerates, but idk if she has much to do with that. Fortunately, she posts her filled lecture slides and streams/records lectures via zoom. Exams are pretty straight forward to prepare for, but are somewhat difficult.

The class is insanely easy and the prof is nice. Lectures are the most boring thing ever, but you can live through them. Midterm and Final were discussed 2 weeks before they were given, which gives you plenty of time to practice. Overall easy course.
giving them a packet yo

I think Atousa is personally a very nice prof, but like other have mentioned, I could not for the life of me learn from her lectures in this class. To study for the final, I watched videos from a different school and everything made sense easily. Also based on the course description, we only covered about half the course's material.

I mean I basically learned on my own, the tests are like the homework but a little different and more advanced. Final was okay, about expected difficulty. For a 3rd year engineering level course though, I would expect to learn something during lectures but I'm always left confused. Would not take again, glad it's over..

The class is fair, and material isn't too hard, but Atousa isn't great. Notes are messy and lectures mostly repeat the slides. Pop quizzes force attendance. No materials are provided for exams, but grading scheme is generous (Many buffers). Also complains about participation and grades, which is grating. Also has a lot of tangents during lecture.

She's a nice person, I've asked her multiple questions but her delivery could use work, not sure if it's the accent or just reading off slides but I don't understand much until I go home and read myself...MT was straight forward but she doesn't give any material for tests. Class is dead quiet, no one knows what's going on too.
can't wait for the knowledge gap in 320
i refuse to believe that anyone in this class is actually learning

Very easy class. The labs are pretty easy. The midterms and final are given 2 weeks beforehand and help prepare for the actual test itself. The prof is nice, she's very open to answering questions during lecture.

Very easy class. The only way to do poorly is by ignoring the class and not reading instructions. All assignments, midterms, and final, are given ~1-2 weeks beforehand and are very detailed. It practically gives you the answers and deducted marks are usually just because of rounding errors. The prof is nice, people just don't like her accent.
knowledgeable and caring

Most of the grades for this class come from labs. It seems daunting at first, but all the instructions are very detailed, and if you actually read them it's very easy. Unfortunately, your success is dependent on your lab partner which is randomly picked. Lectures (at 8AM btw) are not entirely necessary, as she just goes over the lab in more detail.
Avoid at all costs

The class itself is pretty easy but atousa is such a pain to deal with, a lot of times she comes off as very rude/unapproachable and the TA's sometimes don't know what to do either leaving you to fend for yourself. I remember she insulted me once for asking for clarification because of a concept she couldn't properly explain
Lectures are alright, she gives alot of resources so youll be well prepared for exams. In class quizzes are definitely kind of annoying though
painful lecture, why cant school just find someone who can actually explain things in English

She is not a good prof. You don't know if you will have a normal lecture or get ripped into cause she isn't in a good mood and not understanding in her way. In-class quizzes worth 15% of your mark randomly sprinkled around and horrible examples. If I have the chance to choose between her and Craig (another prof) I choose Craig Scratchly.
"No more than 1 unmarketable tomtato" ahh prof
Avoid at all cost
Easy grader, lots of time to prepare for midterms, which are the exact same as the experiments in class - just different numbers.

This class was very straightforward, Atousa is helpful and nice. Make sure you show up to labs and practice for the midterm and final, and you'll do fine. There is one group project, where you solder components onto a circuit board, but it is marked very generously.

This class was very straightforward and taught me a lot about using electronic instruments such as the DMM, power supply, oscilloscope. There is one group project where you solder some components onto a circuit board, but it is marked very generously. Atousa is helpful. Show up to the labs, practice for the midterm and final and you'll do fine.

She does so many things to make your life easy, like allowing collaboration during quizzes, grace period policy,  and lectures on Zoom with recording if you don't want to go to class. Exams are fair and doable if you prepared for it. Pre-midterm material is almost trivial. Post-midterm material however is surprisingly challenging, so beware.
Ensc 120 is easy so don't worry about the prof. But Atousa can be rude sometimes

Atousa seems like a wonderful person to be around, but she is NOT fit for teaching. ENSC380 is a difficult course and her lectures teach you nothing. Her lecture notes will have the question, minimal work, and the answer and expect you to know how SHE got from A to B. She also can't explain topics to save her life. Avoid this prof.
